title: Basic networking tutorial incorrect podman create network command on MACVLAN needs updating
body:
### Issue Description

Issue Description: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/basic_networking.md#macvlan MACVLAN tutorial section has an incorrect command when creating the macvlan network.





### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:
1.Follow the tutorial for basic networking (macvlan section) https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/basic_networking.md#macvlan
2.  Issue the command `sudo podman network create -d macvlan -o parent=eno1 webnetwork`


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received:
Error: macvlan driver needs at least one subnet specified, DHCP is not yet supported with netavark

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

happened after reseting podman to learn the network stack

comments.body:
suggested resolution:
-----------------------------------

manual page podman-network-create (1) shows an example of how to create a macvlan different from the tutorial example where the --subnet parameter is included

> sudo podman network create -d macvlan -o parent=eno1 --subnet 192.5.0.0/16 webnetwork
gave me the expected result
webnetwork

> sudo podman network inspect webnetwork
[
     {
          "name": "webnetwork",
          "id": "7cc4506f69df330e8789e91b13484a74189f28dfaf3748fb5faf50e0b27004bf",
          "driver": "macvlan",
          "network_interface": "eno1",
          "created": "2023-02-27T16:33:39.168318454+11:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "192.5.0.0/16",
                    "gateway": "192.5.0.1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": false,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": false,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
]
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

podman 4.5 and netavark v1.6 should support DHCP so this will no longer be an issue going forward.
That said it still needs to be updated to reflect the steps that are required for netavark.
